channel.title: TR Key
channel.description: Automatic updates for TR Key — the menu-bar key + BPM detector.

item.title: Version 2.5.0
item.description:
• Tempo is more accurate. Some tracks with a shuffle or half-time feel used to come out at the wrong speed — about two-thirds of the real tempo (a 90 BPM beat reading as 60, a 95 as 63). Those now lock onto the right tempo.
  • Sharper key on real mixes. The key now leans on the bass line to find the true root, so it stops naming a chord a fifth above the real key — a common slip on full productions. More songs land on the exact key, and almost all land in the right key family.
  • Smoother and more reliable while it listens. Tightened the live audio path so it stays rock-steady when you switch outputs (speakers to headphones), uses a touch less CPU, and shuts its audio listening down cleanly when you quit.
  • Still 100% on-device and real-time — nothing leaves your Mac.
  • Notarized Developer ID build (Capiscana, Inc.).

item.title: Version 2.4.0
item.description:
• Big accuracy upgrade. On real tracks the key now lands right far more often — the previous build could name the relative major instead of the actual minor (so an A minor track read as C major, or a song could come out a fifth off). On a set of known-key reference tracks, exact matches went from 3 in 8 to 6 in 8, and it's in the right key family on 7 of 8.
  • Registers a key faster — within about 3 seconds of audio, matching Mixed In Key, instead of sitting on "…" or never locking.
  • Still 100% on-device and real-time — nothing leaves your Mac.
  • Notarized Developer ID build (Capiscana, Inc.).

item.title: Version 2.3.3
item.description:
• Rebuilt key detection from the ground up. The whole analysis chain — not just one piece — now follows the same approach Session Vault uses, so the key you see here lines up with Vault (and Mixed In Key) on real tracks far more often. Earlier versions could be off by a whole step or a fifth; that's fixed.
  • Much faster — a key now locks in a second or two instead of dragging.
  • Renamed to TR Key.
  • New Reset Detection menu item — clears the current reading so it re-locks on whatever's playing now (handy when you switch tracks).
  • Light enough to leave running all day (near-zero CPU when nothing's playing). Still 100% on-device — nothing leaves your Mac.
  • Notarized Developer ID build (Capiscana, Inc.).

item.title: Version 2.3.2
item.description:
• Sharper key detection: TR Key/BPM now matches Session Vault's key exactly, including on tracks where the two used to disagree. If you've ever seen a key here that didn't match what Vault (or Mixed In Key) said, this is the fix.
  • Same on-device, real-time key + BPM engine — nothing leaves your Mac.
  • Notarized Developer ID build (Capiscana, Inc.).

item.title: Version 2.3.1
item.description:
• TR Key/BPM now checks for updates automatically — on launch and hourly — so you always know when there's a new version. It prompts you; it never installs anything on its own.
  • Same on-device key + BPM accuracy (Essentia-grade harmonic-profile engine, matched to Session Vault).
  • Notarized Developer ID build (Capiscana, Inc.).

item.title: Version 2.3.0
item.description:
• On-device key detection now matches Session Vault exactly (Essentia-grade harmonic-profile key engine).
  • Live system-audio key + BPM in the menu bar — reads your Mac's output mix via the CoreAudio process-tap (no Screen Recording permission).
  • Tighter BPM octave handling closes the half/double-time trap.
  • Menu-bar title in Tommy's exact format (e.g. "Ebm 128").
  • Notarized Developer ID build (Capiscana, Inc.).

item.title: Version 1.0
item.description:
• First Sparkle-enabled release of TR Key/BPM.
  • On-device key + BPM detection from any audio file.
  • Notarized Developer ID build (Capiscana, Inc.).
